answer:
\boxed{C}

John von Neumann is widely recognized as one of the founders of game theory, particularly for his work on zero-sum games and the concept of Nash equilibrium (though Nash later formalized it). While Leonhard Euler made contributions to combinatorics and probability, his work is not directly tied to modern game theory. Stefan Banach and Carl Friedrich Gauss are more associated with functional analysis and number theory, respectively.
